note: Curated sources a nephrologist actually cites. Snippets are short, paraphrased summaries for retrieval + display; the identifier/url point to the authoritative source the clinician should confirm against. Extend per specialty (add cardiology.v1.json) without code changes.

citations.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of CKD
citations.section: Ch. 3–4: evaluation, risk (KFRE), and delaying progression
citations.snippet: Stage CKD by cause, GFR (G1–G5) and albuminuria (A1–A3); use the KFRE for risk. RAS inhibition and an SGLT2 inhibitor are foundational for albuminuric CKD to slow progression.

citations.title: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in CKD
citations.section: SGLT2i, metformin dosing, finerenone, RAS blockade
citations.snippet: For T2D + CKD: metformin + an SGLT2 inhibitor as first-line where eGFR permits; add finerenone for residual albuminuria on a maximally tolerated RAS inhibitor with acceptable potassium. Metformin: reassess dose at eGFR <45, stop <30.

citations.title: KDIGO 2021 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Management of Glomerular Diseases
citations.section: IgAN, membranous, RPGN/ANCA, anti-GBM, FSGS, lupus nephritis
citations.snippet: IgAN: optimize supportive care (RAS blockade ± SGLT2i) before immunosuppression. RPGN: do not delay biopsy or empiric therapy; ANCA and anti-GBM require prompt immunosuppression, with plasma exchange for anti-GBM and selected severe ANCA.

citations.title: KDIGO 2017 Update: Chronic Kidney Disease–Mineral and Bone Disorder (CKD-MBD)
citations.section: Phosphate, PTH, calcium, vitamin D, calcimimetics
citations.snippet: Lower elevated phosphate toward normal, avoiding hypercalcemia; prefer non-calcium binders when calcium load is a concern. For high PTH on dialysis, calcimimetics, calcitriol/analogs, or a combination — individualized to calcium and phosphate.

citations.title: FIDELIO-DKD: Finerenone in CKD and Type 2 Diabetes
citations.snippet: In T2D + CKD on a maximally tolerated RAS inhibitor, finerenone reduced CKD progression and cardiovascular events versus placebo; hyperkalemia was more frequent, mandating potassium monitoring and eGFR-guided starting dose.

citations.title: FIGARO-DKD: Cardiovascular Events with Finerenone in CKD and Type 2 Diabetes
citations.snippet: Finerenone reduced cardiovascular outcomes across a broader, earlier CKD + T2D population, complementing FIDELIO's kidney findings; the pooled FIDELITY analysis supports kidney and CV benefit.

citations.title: DAPA-CKD: Dapagliflozin in Patients with Chronic Kidney Disease
citations.section: SGLT2i in CKD with and without diabetes
citations.snippet: Dapagliflozin reduced the composite of sustained eGFR decline, ESKD, and death in CKD with or without type 2 diabetes, including IgA nephropathy, down to an eGFR of 25.

citations.title: CREDENCE: Canagliflozin and Renal Outcomes in T2D and Nephropathy
citations.section: Kidney and CV outcomes in albuminuric diabetic CKD
citations.snippet: In T2D with albuminuric CKD, canagliflozin lowered the risk of kidney failure and cardiovascular events; established SGLT2 inhibition as kidney-protective beyond glycemic control.

citations.title: EMPA-KIDNEY: Empagliflozin in Patients with Chronic Kidney Disease
citations.section: Broad CKD population, lower eGFR range
citations.snippet: Empagliflozin reduced progression of kidney disease or cardiovascular death across a broad CKD population, including lower eGFR and non-diabetic causes, broadening SGLT2i eligibility.

citations.title: KERENDIA (finerenone) FDA Prescribing Information
citations.section: Dosing by eGFR; potassium thresholds; contraindications
citations.snippet: Start finerenone 10 mg (eGFR 25–<60) or 20 mg (eGFR ≥60); do not start if serum potassium >5.0 mmol/L. Check potassium at ~4 weeks and adjust; contraindicated with strong CYP3A4 inhibitors and adrenal insufficiency.

citations.title: ELIQUIS (apixaban) FDA Prescribing Information
citations.snippet: For nonvalvular AF, reduce apixaban to 2.5 mg BID when ≥2 of: age ≥80, weight ≤60 kg, serum creatinine ≥1.5 mg/dL. Note eGFR is NOT one of the three criteria; the label addresses use across renal function including dialysis.

citations.title: PROTECT: Sparsentan versus Irbesartan in IgA Nephropathy
citations.snippet: Sparsentan reduced proteinuria versus irbesartan in IgA nephropathy with a manageable safety profile, supporting a targeted option beyond RAS blockade for high-risk IgAN.

citations.title: NefIgArd: Targeted-Release Budesonide in Primary IgA Nephropathy
citations.snippet: Targeted-release budesonide (Nefecon) reduced proteinuria and preserved eGFR over two years in primary IgA nephropathy at risk of progression, a mucosal-immunity–directed therapy.

citations.title: ISPD Peritonitis Guideline (2022 Update)
citations.snippet: For PD peritonitis with cloudy effluent, start empiric intraperitoneal antibiotics covering gram-positive (vancomycin or cefazolin) and gram-negative (aminoglycoside or ceftazidime) organisms after cultures; remove the catheter for refractory, relapsing, or fungal peritonitis.

citations.title: Clinical Practice Guideline on Diagnosis and Treatment of Hyponatraemia (EU)
citations.snippet: Limit sodium correction to ~10 mmol/L in the first 24 h (lower in high-risk chronic hyponatremia) to avoid osmotic demyelination; for overcorrection, re-lower with electrolyte-free water ± desmopressin. Severe symptomatic hyponatremia: 3% saline boluses.

citations.title: Hyperkalemia in CKD/HD — acute management principles
citations.snippet: For hyperkalemia with ECG changes, give IV calcium to stabilize the myocardium (does NOT lower potassium), shift with insulin+dextrose ± beta-agonist, then remove via dialysis in ESRD. On HD, avoid an abrupt 1K bath because a steep K+ gradient is arrhythmogenic.

citations.title: KDIGO 2024 CKD — Definition & staging (CGA)
citations.snippet: Define CKD as abnormalities of kidney structure or function present >3 months. Stage by Cause, GFR category (G1–G5), and Albuminuria category (A1–A3); the CGA heatmap grades risk of progression, AKI, and CV events.

citations.title: KDIGO 2024 CKD — Kidney Failure Risk Equation (KFRE)
citations.section: Chapter 2: risk prediction and referral thresholds
citations.snippet: Use the validated Kidney Failure Risk Equation to estimate 2- and 5-year risk of kidney failure; a 5-year risk >3–5% informs nephrology referral and multidisciplinary care planning.

citations.title: KDIGO 2024 CKD — SGLT2 inhibitors
citations.snippet: Recommend an SGLT2 inhibitor for adults with CKD and eGFR ≥20 with albuminuria, with or without diabetes; continue until dialysis/transplant. Expect an initial reversible eGFR dip; do not stop for that dip.

citations.title: KDIGO 2024 CKD — RAS inhibition
citations.snippet: Use ACEi or ARB (not both) titrated to the maximum tolerated dose for albuminuric CKD; tolerate a creatinine rise up to ~30% and monitor potassium. Do not routinely stop RASi at low eGFR solely to defer dialysis.

citations.title: KDIGO 2024 CKD — Blood pressure target
citations.snippet: Target a standardized office systolic blood pressure <120 mmHg in most adults with CKD when tolerated, using proper standardized measurement; individualize for frailty, orthostasis, and polypharmacy.

citations.title: KDIGO 2024 CKD — Lipid management
citations.snippet: Start statin or statin/ezetimibe in adults ≥50 with CKD not on dialysis; do not initiate statins de novo in dialysis-dependent patients but continue if already tolerated (fire-and-forget, no LDL target).

citations.title: KDIGO 2024 CKD — Protein & sodium
citations.snippet: Suggest protein intake ~0.8 g/kg/day in adults with CKD (avoid high protein), and sodium <2 g/day (<5 g NaCl) to help control blood pressure and volume; individualize with dietitian support.

citations.title: KDIGO 2024 CKD — Metformin dosing by eGFR
citations.snippet: Metformin is appropriate in CKD; reassess dose at eGFR <45 and monitor; discontinue when eGFR <30 given lactic-acidosis risk. Hold around iodinated contrast and acute illness.

citations.title: KDIGO 2022 Diabetes-CKD — Finerenone
citations.snippet: Add finerenone (a nonsteroidal MRA) for T2D + CKD with residual albuminuria on a maximally tolerated RASi and normal-ish potassium; monitor potassium and eGFR after initiation.

citations.title: KDIGO 2022 Diabetes-CKD — GLP-1 RA
citations.section: GLP-1 receptor agonists for glycemia + weight
citations.snippet: Prefer a long-acting GLP-1 receptor agonist when additional glucose lowering is needed in T2D + CKD, for glycemic control, weight, and cardiovascular benefit; it can be used at low eGFR.

citations.title: KDIGO 2022 Diabetes-CKD — Glycemic targets
citations.snippet: Individualize HbA1c targets from <6.5% to <8.0% based on hypoglycemia risk, comorbidity, life expectancy, and CKD severity; HbA1c is less reliable at low eGFR and on dialysis.

citations.title: KDIGO 2021 GN — IgA nephropathy
citations.section: Supportive care first; immunosuppression by risk
citations.snippet: Optimize supportive care (max-tolerated RASi ± SGLT2i, BP control) for ≥3 months before considering glucocorticoids in high-risk IgAN; weigh systemic steroid toxicity and consider gut-directed budesonide.

citations.title: KDIGO 2021 GN — Membranous nephropathy
citations.snippet: Risk-stratify primary membranous nephropathy by proteinuria, eGFR trajectory, and anti-PLA2R titer; treat moderate/high risk with rituximab, cyclophosphamide-based, or CNI regimens rather than immediate immunosuppression in low risk.

citations.title: KDIGO 2021 GN — FSGS
citations.section: Primary vs secondary; steroids for primary
citations.snippet: Distinguish primary (diffuse foot-process effacement, nephrotic) from secondary/genetic FSGS; treat primary FSGS with high-dose glucocorticoids and CNI for steroid resistance; secondary forms get RAS blockade, not immunosuppression.

citations.title: KDIGO 2021 GN — Minimal change disease
citations.snippet: Treat adult minimal change disease with high-dose glucocorticoids; use CNI, cyclophosphamide, or rituximab for frequent relapse or steroid dependence.

citations.title: KDIGO 2021 GN — Lupus nephritis
citations.snippet: For active class III/IV lupus nephritis, induce with MMF or low-dose IV cyclophosphamide plus glucocorticoids, increasingly with belimumab or a CNI (voclosporin) added; maintain with MMF or azathioprine.

citations.title: KDIGO 2021 GN — ANCA vasculitis
citations.snippet: Treat ANCA-associated GN promptly with glucocorticoids plus rituximab or cyclophosphamide; consider plasma exchange for severe kidney disease or diffuse alveolar hemorrhage. Reduced-dose steroid regimens (PEXIVAS) limit toxicity.

citations.title: KDIGO 2021 GN — Anti-GBM disease
citations.snippet: Anti-GBM (Goodpasture) disease is an emergency: start plasma exchange plus glucocorticoids and cyclophosphamide without delay; dialysis-dependence at presentation with 100% crescents portends poor renal recovery.

citations.title: KDIGO 2021 GN — C3 glomerulopathy / MPGN
citations.snippet: Evaluate MPGN pattern for infection, monoclonal gammopathy, and complement dysregulation; C3 glomerulopathy warrants a complement workup, and treatment ranges from RAS blockade to immunosuppression and emerging complement inhibitors.

citations.title: KDIGO 2021 GN — Infection-related GN
citations.snippet: Manage infection-related glomerulonephritis by treating the underlying infection (including endocarditis and shunt infections); immunosuppression is generally avoided unless a separate primary GN is proven.

citations.title: KDIGO 2017 CKD-MBD — Phosphate
citations.snippet: Lower elevated serum phosphate toward the normal range; restrict dietary phosphate and use binders, limiting calcium-based binders when hypercalcemia, vascular calcification, or adynamic bone are concerns.

citations.title: KDIGO 2017 CKD-MBD — PTH / SHPT
citations.snippet: For rising or persistently high PTH on dialysis, use calcimimetics (cinacalcet, etelcalcetide), calcitriol/analogs, or combinations; avoid oversuppression causing adynamic bone.

citations.title: KDIGO 2021 Blood Pressure in CKD
citations.snippet: Target standardized office SBP <120 mmHg in CKD not on dialysis when tolerated; RAS inhibitors are preferred for albuminuric CKD and diabetic kidney disease.

citations.title: KDIGO 2012 Acute Kidney Injury
citations.snippet: Stage AKI by serum creatinine rise and urine output; prevent with volume optimization and nephrotoxin avoidance; there is no benefit to routine early dialysis — start RRT for refractory volume, electrolyte, or uremic indications.

citations.title: KDIGO 2012 Anemia in CKD
citations.snippet: Do not target normal hemoglobin with ESAs; individualize, generally not exceeding Hb 11.5 g/dL, and address iron deficiency first. Higher Hb targets increase stroke and thrombosis risk (TREAT, CHOIR, CREATE).

citations.title: KDIGO 2020 Transplant Candidate Evaluation
citations.snippet: Evaluate transplant candidates for cardiovascular risk, malignancy, infection, and immunologic risk; most patients with ESKD benefit from transplantation, including preemptive listing at eGFR <20.

citations.title: KDIGO Kidney Transplant Recipient Care
citations.snippet: Maintain most kidney transplant recipients on a CNI (tacrolimus) plus mycophenolate ± steroids; monitor drug levels, screen for infection (CMV, BK) and malignancy, and manage cardiovascular and metabolic complications.

citations.title: KDIGO 2018 Hepatitis C in CKD
citations.snippet: Treat hepatitis C with direct-acting antivirals in CKD; pangenotypic regimens (glecaprevir/pibrentasvir) are safe across eGFR including dialysis, enabling HCV-positive donor-to-recipient transplantation.

citations.title: KDIGO 2017 Living Kidney Donor Evaluation
citations.snippet: Assess living donor candidates for individualized lifetime kidney-failure risk (age, GFR, albuminuria, BP, BMI, family history) rather than fixed thresholds; ensure informed consent about long-term risk.

citations.title: KDIGO Diabetes-CKD — Blood pressure & RAS
citations.section: BP control with RAS inhibition in DKD
citations.snippet: In diabetic kidney disease with albuminuria, use a RAS inhibitor titrated to maximum tolerated dose as the antihypertensive backbone; combine with SGLT2i and finerenone for kidney and CV protection.

citations.title: DAPA-HF: Dapagliflozin in Heart Failure
citations.section: SGLT2i in HFrEF incl. reduced eGFR
citations.snippet: Dapagliflozin reduced worsening heart failure and cardiovascular death in HFrEF regardless of diabetes, with benefit preserved down to eGFR 30 — relevant to cardiorenal patients on nephrology service.

citations.title: EMPEROR-Reduced: Empagliflozin in HFrEF
citations.snippet: Empagliflozin reduced heart-failure hospitalization and slowed eGFR decline in HFrEF, reinforcing SGLT2 inhibition across the cardiorenal spectrum.

citations.title: SPRINT: Intensive vs Standard BP Control
citations.snippet: Intensive systolic BP control (<120 mmHg) reduced cardiovascular events and mortality versus <140, including in the CKD subgroup, at the cost of more AKI and electrolyte abnormalities.

citations.title: IDNT: Irbesartan in Diabetic Nephropathy
citations.snippet: Irbesartan slowed progression of type 2 diabetic nephropathy independent of blood pressure, establishing ARB renoprotection in albuminuric diabetic CKD.

citations.title: RENAAL: Losartan in Type 2 Diabetic Nephropathy
citations.snippet: Losartan reduced the doubling of creatinine and progression to end-stage kidney disease in type 2 diabetic nephropathy, independent of blood-pressure effects.

citations.title: ALTITUDE: Aliskiren added to RAS blockade
citations.snippet: Adding aliskiren to an ACEi or ARB in type 2 diabetes increased hyperkalemia, hypotension, and adverse events without benefit — evidence against dual RAS blockade.

citations.title: ONTARGET: Telmisartan, Ramipril, or Both
citations.snippet: Combined ACEi plus ARB increased hypotension, hyperkalemia, and renal dysfunction without cardiovascular benefit versus monotherapy — do not routinely combine RAS blockers.

citations.title: STOP-ACEi: Continuing vs Stopping RASi in Advanced CKD
citations.snippet: Stopping ACEi/ARB in advanced CKD did not preserve eGFR or delay kidney failure versus continuing — do not routinely withdraw RAS inhibition solely because eGFR is low.

citations.title: SHARP: Simvastatin/Ezetimibe in CKD
citations.snippet: Simvastatin plus ezetimibe reduced major atherosclerotic events in CKD including dialysis patients, with the largest relative benefit in non-dialysis CKD.

citations.title: TREAT: Darbepoetin in Diabetic CKD Anemia
citations.snippet: Targeting near-normal hemoglobin with darbepoetin in diabetic CKD did not reduce death or CV/renal events and nearly doubled stroke risk — supporting conservative ESA Hb targets.

citations.title: CHOIR: Higher vs Lower Hb Target with Epoetin
citations.section: Higher Hb target harms in CKD
citations.snippet: Targeting Hb 13.5 vs 11.3 g/dL with epoetin alfa in non-dialysis CKD increased composite death and CV events — one basis for conservative anemia targets.

citations.title: CANVAS: Canagliflozin CV & Renal Outcomes
citations.snippet: Canagliflozin reduced cardiovascular events and slowed albuminuria progression in type 2 diabetes but carried an amputation and fracture signal in this program.

citations.title: DECLARE-TIMI 58: Dapagliflozin CV Outcomes
citations.snippet: Dapagliflozin reduced heart-failure hospitalization and a renal composite across a broad type 2 diabetes population, including primary-prevention patients.

citations.title: CREDENCE (kidney primary endpoint)
citations.snippet: In type 2 diabetes with albuminuric CKD, canagliflozin lowered kidney failure and CV events — the first dedicated SGLT2i renal-outcome trial with a kidney primary endpoint.

citations.title: FLOW: Semaglutide in Diabetic CKD
citations.snippet: Semaglutide reduced major kidney disease events and cardiovascular death in type 2 diabetes with CKD, extending GLP-1 receptor agonist benefit to kidney outcomes.

citations.title: PEXIVAS: Plasma Exchange & Steroid Dosing in ANCA
citations.snippet: In severe ANCA vasculitis, plasma exchange did not reduce death or ESKD, and a reduced-dose glucocorticoid regimen was noninferior for these outcomes with less infection.

citations.title: ADVOCATE: Avacopan in ANCA Vasculitis
citations.snippet: Avacopan, an oral C5a receptor antagonist, was superior for sustained remission at week 52 and enabled glucocorticoid sparing in ANCA-associated vasculitis.

citations.title: EMPA-KIDNEY (broad CKD)
citations.snippet: Empagliflozin reduced kidney disease progression or CV death across a broad CKD population including low eGFR and non-diabetic causes.

citations.title: DUPLEX: Sparsentan in FSGS
citations.snippet: Sparsentan reduced proteinuria versus irbesartan in focal segmental glomerulosclerosis, though the eGFR-slope primary endpoint was not statistically met.

citations.title: APPLAUSE-IgAN: Iptacopan in IgA Nephropathy
citations.snippet: Iptacopan, an oral factor B inhibitor of the alternative complement pathway, reduced proteinuria in primary IgA nephropathy — a complement-targeted option.

citations.title: JARDIANCE (empagliflozin) FDA Label
citations.snippet: Empagliflozin is indicated to reduce CKD progression; initiation guidance spans low eGFR. Counsel on volume depletion, genital mycotic infection, euglycemic DKA, and holding around surgery/acute illness.

citations.title: FARXIGA (dapagliflozin) FDA Label
citations.snippet: Dapagliflozin is indicated to reduce risk of eGFR decline, ESKD, CV death, and HF hospitalization in CKD; not for initiation with an eGFR below the labeled threshold for glycemic indication. Watch euglycemic DKA.

citations.title: LOKELMA (sodium zirconium cyclosilicate) FDA Label
citations.snippet: Sodium zirconium cyclosilicate lowers serum potassium in hyperkalemia; not for emergency life-threatening hyperkalemia. Separate dosing from other oral drugs by hours and monitor for edema from sodium load.

citations.title: VELTASSA (patiromer) FDA Label
citations.snippet: Patiromer binds potassium in the GI tract for chronic hyperkalemia; delayed onset (not for emergencies), separate from other oral medications by ≥3 hours, and monitor magnesium.

citations.title: PROGRAF (tacrolimus) FDA Label
citations.snippet: Tacrolimus is a calcineurin inhibitor for transplant rejection prophylaxis; narrow therapeutic index requires trough monitoring, with nephrotoxicity, neurotoxicity, hyperglycemia, and CYP3A drug interactions.

citations.title: CELLCEPT (mycophenolate) FDA Label
citations.snippet: Mycophenolate mofetil prevents transplant rejection and treats several glomerular diseases; teratogenic (REMS/pregnancy prevention), with GI intolerance and cytopenias.

citations.title: SPS (sodium polystyrene sulfonate) FDA Label
citations.snippet: Sodium polystyrene sulfonate lowers potassium slowly; boxed concern for intestinal necrosis, avoid with sorbitol and in bowel dysmotility/post-op ileus — not first-line for acute hyperkalemia.

citations.title: Apixaban in Advanced CKD & Dialysis
citations.section: Renal dosing nuance beyond the label
citations.snippet: For NVAF, apixaban 2.5 mg BID requires ≥2 of: age ≥80, weight ≤60 kg, creatinine ≥1.5 mg/dL — eGFR is NOT a criterion. Use in dialysis is guided by label pharmacokinetics and limited outcome data.

citations.title: Acute Hyperkalemia — Emergency Management
citations.snippet: With ECG changes or K+ >6.5, give IV calcium to stabilize the myocardium (does NOT lower K+), shift with insulin+dextrose ± nebulized beta-agonist, then remove via dialysis in ESKD; binders are for chronic control, not emergencies.

citations.title: Chronic Hyponatremia & SIADH
citations.snippet: Confirm true hypotonic hyponatremia, assess volume status and urine osmolality/sodium; SIADH is euvolemic with concentrated urine. Correct chronic hyponatremia ≤8 mmol/L per 24 h to avoid osmotic demyelination.

citations.title: Hypernatremia — Free Water Deficit
citations.snippet: Correct hypernatremia by replacing the calculated free-water deficit, lowering sodium ≤10–12 mmol/L per 24 h to avoid cerebral edema; treat the underlying cause (diabetes insipidus, insensible losses, hypertonic gains).

citations.title: Metabolic Acidosis in CKD
citations.snippet: Treat metabolic acidosis in CKD (serum bicarbonate <22 mmol/L) with oral alkali to maintain bicarbonate in the normal range; correcting acidosis may slow CKD progression and preserve muscle/bone.

citations.title: Renal Tubular Acidosis — Typing
citations.snippet: Classify RTA by serum potassium and urine pH: distal (type 1) with high urine pH and hypokalemia; proximal (type 2) with bicarbonate wasting; type 4 (hyperkalemic) from aldosterone deficiency/resistance, common in diabetic CKD.

citations.title: Diabetic Ketoacidosis — Fluids & Potassium
citations.snippet: In DKA, restore volume, give insulin, and track potassium closely: total-body potassium is depleted even when serum is normal/high, so replace potassium before/with insulin once K+ <5.2 to avoid dangerous hypokalemia.

citations.title: Cardiorenal Syndrome — Decongestion
citations.snippet: In acute cardiorenal syndrome, effective decongestion often transiently raises creatinine (hemoconcentration) without true injury; continue loop diuretics with adequate dosing and sequential nephron blockade rather than stopping for the creatinine bump.

citations.title: Contrast-Associated AKI
citations.snippet: Contrast-associated AKI risk is lower than historically feared; for higher-risk patients (low eGFR, volume depletion) provide IV isotonic fluid, avoid volume depletion, and do not withhold clearly indicated contrast studies.

citations.title: Rhabdomyolysis-Associated AKI
citations.snippet: Treat rhabdomyolysis with early aggressive isotonic fluids to maintain urine output; monitor creatine kinase, potassium, phosphate, and calcium — hypocalcemia early and hypercalcemia during recovery.

citations.title: Tumor Lysis Syndrome
citations.snippet: Prevent tumor lysis syndrome with hydration and urate-lowering (allopurinol prophylaxis, rasburicase for high risk); manage hyperkalemia, hyperphosphatemia, and hypocalcemia; avoid alkalinization when phosphate is high.

citations.title: ADPKD — Tolvaptan
citations.snippet: Tolvaptan slows eGFR decline and total kidney volume growth in rapidly progressing ADPKD; requires monitoring for hepatotoxicity and managing aquaresis (thirst, polyuria).

citations.title: Monoclonal Gammopathy of Renal Significance
citations.snippet: A monoclonal gammopathy causing kidney disease (MGRS) warrants hematologic evaluation and clone-directed therapy even without meeting myeloma criteria; kidney biopsy defines the lesion (e.g., amyloid, MIDD, proliferative GN with deposits).

citations.title: Myeloma Cast Nephropathy — AKI
citations.snippet: In myeloma with AKI from cast nephropathy, start clone-directed therapy urgently (bortezomib-based) and hydrate; the role of extracorporeal light-chain removal is uncertain and not routinely recommended.

citations.title: Hemodialysis Adequacy (Kt/V)
citations.snippet: Target a single-pool Kt/V of ~1.4 (minimum 1.2) per session for thrice-weekly hemodialysis; individualize with residual kidney function, and avoid large ultrafiltration rates that cause intradialytic hypotension.

citations.title: ISPD PD Adequacy
citations.snippet: Prescribe peritoneal dialysis to achieve adequate solute clearance and volume control while preserving residual kidney function; a fixed Kt/V target is less emphasized than symptoms, volume, and quality of life.

citations.title: Vascular Access for Hemodialysis
citations.snippet: Favor an arteriovenous fistula for durable hemodialysis access using a patient-centered ESKD Life-Plan; minimize tunneled catheters given infection and central-stenosis risk.

citations.title: Nephrolithiasis — Metabolic Prevention
citations.snippet: Prevent recurrent kidney stones with high fluid intake (urine output >2.5 L/day), dietary sodium restriction, adequate dietary calcium, and thiazides/citrate guided by 24-hour urine metabolic profiling.

citations.title: Gout in CKD — Urate Lowering
citations.snippet: Treat-to-target urate <6 mg/dL with allopurinol as first-line even in CKD, starting low and titrating (do not fix the dose by creatinine clearance); febuxostat is an alternative with a CV caution.

citations.title: Hypertension & CKD in Pregnancy
citations.snippet: In pregnancy, avoid ACEi/ARB/direct renin inhibitors and SGLT2i; use labetalol, nifedipine, or methyldopa. Manage CKD and preeclampsia risk with low-dose aspirin and close BP and proteinuria monitoring.

citations.title: Nephrotoxin Stewardship in AKI
citations.snippet: Reduce AKI by holding nephrotoxins (NSAIDs, aminoglycosides, high-dose vancomycin with piperacillin-tazobactam, RAS inhibitors during acute illness) and adjusting renally-cleared drug doses; review the medication list at each eGFR change.

citations.title: Complicated UTI / Pyelonephritis
citations.snippet: Treat complicated UTI/pyelonephritis with agents active against likely uropathogens, adjusting doses for eGFR; avoid nitrofurantoin at low eGFR and image for obstruction in the setting of AKI or sepsis.

citations.title: Hepatorenal Syndrome — Terlipressin
citations.snippet: Terlipressin with albumin improves reversal of hepatorenal syndrome-AKI versus albumin alone, at a cost of respiratory and ischemic adverse events; select patients carefully and monitor volume status.

citations.title: Resistant Hypertension — Spironolactone
citations.snippet: Spironolactone is the most effective add-on for resistant hypertension (PATHWAY-2); monitor potassium and eGFR, using caution/ dose reduction in CKD given hyperkalemia risk.

citations.title: Bicarbonate in Severe Metabolic Acidosis (AKI)
citations.snippet: In severe metabolic acidemia with AKI (AKIN 2–3), IV sodium bicarbonate reduced the need for dialysis and improved outcomes in a subgroup (BICAR-ICU) — consider it for severe acidosis with kidney injury.

citations.title: Phosphate Binder Selection
citations.snippet: Prefer non-calcium phosphate binders (sevelamer, lanthanum, iron-based) when vascular calcification, hypercalcemia, or adynamic bone are concerns; balance pill burden, cost, and GI tolerability.

citations.title: Iron Therapy in CKD Anemia
citations.snippet: Correct iron deficiency in CKD anemia before or alongside ESAs; IV iron is often preferred in dialysis. Use TSAT and ferritin to guide, avoiding iron when TSAT and ferritin are already high (PIVOTAL supports proactive IV iron in HD).

citations.title: RASi & Hyperkalemia Management
citations.snippet: To maintain guideline RAS inhibition in CKD/HF despite hyperkalemia, add a potassium binder (patiromer, SZC), review dietary potassium and other K-raising drugs, rather than reflexively down-titrating RASi.

citations.title: Albuminuria Screening (UACR)
citations.snippet: Quantify albuminuria with a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio on an early-morning sample; confirm a positive result and use albuminuria category (A1–A3) alongside eGFR for risk staging and therapy decisions.

citations.title: eGFR Estimation & Cystatin C
citations.snippet: Use the race-free 2021 CKD-EPI creatinine equation; confirm with cystatin C (eGFRcr-cys) when a precise estimate affects drug dosing or eligibility, or when creatinine is unreliable (low/high muscle mass).

citations.title: KDIGO 2024 CKD — Nephrology referral criteria
citations.snippet: Refer to nephrology for eGFR <30, sustained UACR ≥300 mg/g, rapid eGFR decline, refractory hypertension or hyperkalemia, recurrent stones, or hereditary kidney disease; earlier referral improves preparation for kidney replacement.

citations.title: KDIGO 2024 CKD — Potassium management
citations.snippet: Manage hyperkalemia in CKD with diet counseling, review of K-raising drugs, loop diuretics, and potassium binders so that guideline RAS inhibition can continue rather than being down-titrated or stopped.

citations.title: KDIGO Diabetes-CKD — Self-monitoring & CGM
citations.snippet: Use continuous glucose monitoring or structured self-monitoring in insulin-treated diabetes with CKD, since HbA1c underestimates glycemia at low eGFR and on dialysis where red-cell turnover is altered.

citations.title: IgAN — International Risk Prediction Tool
citations.snippet: Combine clinical (eGFR, BP, proteinuria) and MEST-C histology to estimate IgA nephropathy progression risk; persistent proteinuria >1 g/day despite optimized supportive care marks high risk.

citations.title: Membranous — Anti-PLA2R Monitoring
citations.snippet: Track anti-PLA2R antibody titers to guide and monitor treatment response in primary membranous nephropathy; immunologic remission (falling titer) precedes proteinuria remission and informs therapy duration.

citations.title: Nephrotic Syndrome — VTE Prophylaxis
citations.snippet: Consider prophylactic anticoagulation in nephrotic syndrome with severe hypoalbuminemia (albumin <2–2.5 g/dL), especially membranous nephropathy, weighing the elevated venous thromboembolism risk against bleeding.

citations.title: Transplant — BK Virus Nephropathy
citations.snippet: Screen kidney transplant recipients for BK viremia; rising viral load prompts reduction of immunosuppression as first-line management to prevent BK nephropathy and graft loss.

citations.title: Transplant — CMV Prevention
citations.snippet: Prevent CMV disease in kidney transplant recipients with valganciclovir prophylaxis or preemptive monitoring based on donor/recipient serostatus; D+/R- is the highest-risk mismatch.

citations.title: Transplant — Acute Rejection
citations.snippet: Distinguish T-cell-mediated rejection (treated with steroids ± thymoglobulin) from antibody-mediated rejection (donor-specific antibodies, C4d, microvascular inflammation) needing plasma exchange, IVIG, and adjuncts.

citations.title: Transplant — Recurrent FSGS
citations.snippet: Primary FSGS can recur early post-transplant with heavy proteinuria; plasmapheresis and rituximab are used, and a circulating permeability factor is implicated. Distinguish from de novo or secondary FSGS.

citations.title: Atypical HUS — Complement Inhibition
citations.snippet: Eculizumab controls complement-mediated thrombotic microangiopathy (atypical HUS), improving hematologic and renal outcomes; distinguish aHUS from TTP (ADAMTS13) and shiga-toxin HUS.

citations.title: TTP — ADAMTS13 & Plasma Exchange
citations.snippet: Suspected TTP (severe ADAMTS13 deficiency) needs urgent plasma exchange plus immunosuppression; caplacizumab shortens time to platelet recovery. Do not delay PLEX awaiting confirmation in a TMA with organ injury.

citations.title: Scleroderma Renal Crisis
citations.section: ACEi is the treatment, not the risk
citations.snippet: Scleroderma renal crisis presents with accelerated hypertension and AKI/MAHA; ACE inhibitors are the cornerstone treatment (captopril titrated) and should NOT be withheld — a key exception to holding RASi in AKI.

citations.title: Lithium-Induced Kidney Disease
citations.snippet: Chronic lithium causes nephrogenic diabetes insipidus and slowly progressive CKD; monitor eGFR and consider amiloride for lithium-induced NDI, coordinating with psychiatry before changing therapy.

citations.title: Crystalline Nephropathies
citations.snippet: Crystalline nephropathy (acyclovir, methotrexate, sulfadiazine, atazanavir, oxalate, urate) causes AKI via tubular crystal deposition; prevent with hydration, urine flow, and drug/dose adjustment.

citations.title: Acute Interstitial Nephritis
citations.snippet: Suspect acute interstitial nephritis with AKI, sterile pyuria, and eosinophiluria after culprit drugs (PPIs, antibiotics, NSAIDs, checkpoint inhibitors); stop the culprit and consider corticosteroids for persistent injury.

citations.title: Immune Checkpoint Inhibitor AKI
citations.snippet: Immune checkpoint inhibitors cause AKI mainly via acute interstitial nephritis; grade the injury, hold the ICI, start corticosteroids for higher grades, and involve oncology about rechallenge.

citations.title: Distinguishing HRS from ATN
citations.snippet: In cirrhosis with AKI, exclude hypovolemia (albumin challenge), obstruction, and ATN (granular casts, high urine sodium/FENa) before diagnosing hepatorenal syndrome, which is a functional, diagnosis of exclusion.

citations.title: Urinary Indices in AKI
citations.snippet: Use urine microscopy and indices to phenotype AKI: FENa <1% and bland sediment suggest prerenal; granular/muddy-brown casts and FENa >2% suggest ATN. Diuretics confound FENa — use FEUrea instead.

citations.title: Obstructive Uropathy — AKI
citations.snippet: Relieve post-renal obstruction promptly (catheter, nephrostomy, stent) in AKI; anticipate post-obstructive diuresis with volume and electrolyte losses requiring monitoring and replacement.

citations.title: CRRT vs Intermittent HD in AKI
citations.section: RRT modality selection in the ICU
citations.snippet: Choose continuous renal replacement therapy for hemodynamically unstable ICU patients with AKI; CRRT and intermittent HD have similar survival, so tailor to hemodynamics, fluid goals, and resources.

citations.title: Timing of RRT in AKI
citations.snippet: Accelerated (early) initiation of renal replacement therapy in AKI did not improve survival versus a standard, indication-based strategy (STARRT-AKI) and increased dialysis dependence — start RRT for clear indications.

citations.title: Intradialytic Hypotension
citations.snippet: Reduce intradialytic hypotension by limiting ultrafiltration rate (<13 mL/kg/h), lengthening/cooling dialysis, reassessing target weight and antihypertensive timing, and avoiding large interdialytic weight gains.

citations.title: Dialysate Potassium Bath Selection
citations.snippet: Avoid a 1 mEq/L (1K) dialysate bath when serum potassium is only modestly elevated: a steep potassium gradient is arrhythmogenic and linked to sudden death; use a 2K bath and reassess pre-dialysis potassium.

citations.title: Calciphylaxis (CUA)
citations.snippet: Manage calciphylaxis with wound care, sodium thiosulfate, correcting calcium-phosphate product, stopping warfarin and calcium-based binders/vitamin D, and optimizing dialysis; pain control and multidisciplinary care are essential.

citations.title: Uremic Pericarditis
citations.snippet: Uremic pericarditis is an absolute indication for urgent dialysis; intensify hemodialysis (often daily, minimal heparin due to hemorrhagic risk) and monitor for tamponade.

citations.title: Primary Aldosteronism Screening
citations.snippet: Screen for primary aldosteronism (aldosterone-to-renin ratio) in resistant hypertension, hypertension with hypokalemia, or an adrenal incidentaloma; confirm with dynamic testing and consider adrenal vein sampling.

citations.title: Renovascular Hypertension
citations.snippet: Renal artery stenting did not add benefit to medical therapy for atherosclerotic renovascular disease (CORAL); reserve intervention for flash pulmonary edema, refractory hypertension, or rapidly declining function.

citations.title: ADPKD — Genetics & Screening
citations.snippet: ADPKD is usually caused by PKD1 (more severe) or PKD2 variants; use imaging (age-adjusted cyst criteria) or genetic testing for diagnosis and Mayo imaging classification to identify rapid progressors for tolvaptan.

citations.title: Alport Syndrome
citations.snippet: Alport syndrome (COL4A3/4/5) causes hematuria, progressive CKD, sensorineural hearing loss, and lenticonus; early RAS inhibition slows progression, and family/genetic evaluation is recommended.

citations.title: Thin Basement Membrane / Isolated Hematuria
citations.snippet: Evaluate persistent glomerular hematuria (dysmorphic RBCs, acanthocytes) for IgAN, Alport, and thin basement membrane disease; isolated hematuria with normal function and no proteinuria is often benign but warrants monitoring.

citations.title: Fabry Disease Nephropathy
citations.snippet: Fabry disease (alpha-galactosidase A deficiency) causes proteinuric CKD with characteristic podocyte inclusions; enzyme replacement or chaperone therapy plus RAS blockade addresses renal involvement.

citations.title: Diabetic Kidney Disease — Four-Pillar Therapy
citations.snippet: Layer the four pillars of diabetic kidney disease therapy — maximally tolerated RAS inhibitor, SGLT2 inhibitor, nonsteroidal MRA (finerenone), and GLP-1 receptor agonist — for additive kidney and cardiovascular protection.

citations.title: Hypomagnesemia — Causes & Repletion
citations.snippet: Evaluate hypomagnesemia for GI losses, PPIs, diuretics, calcineurin inhibitors, and EGFR-antibody or cisplatin tubular wasting; repletion aids refractory hypokalemia and hypocalcemia.

citations.title: Hypercalcemia — Acute Management
citations.snippet: Treat significant hypercalcemia with isotonic saline volume repletion, then bisphosphonate or denosumab; add calcitonin for rapid short-term lowering and treat the cause (PTH-driven vs malignancy vs granulomatous).

citations.title: Hypophosphatemia & Refeeding
citations.snippet: Severe hypophosphatemia (refeeding, DKA recovery, alcohol use, proximal tubulopathy) risks respiratory and cardiac dysfunction; replete IV phosphate for symptomatic or very low levels and treat the cause.

citations.title: SGLT2 Inhibitors — Euglycemic DKA
citations.snippet: SGLT2 inhibitors can cause euglycemic diabetic ketoacidosis (near-normal glucose); check ketones with acidosis, hold the drug during acute illness/surgery/low intake, and counsel patients on sick-day rules.

citations.title: Gadolinium & NSF Risk
citations.snippet: Nephrogenic systemic fibrosis risk from gadolinium is very low with newer group II macrocyclic agents even at low eGFR/dialysis; use the lowest necessary dose and shared decision-making rather than blanket avoidance.

citations.title: Primary Hyperoxaluria
citations.snippet: Primary hyperoxaluria type 1 causes recurrent stones, nephrocalcinosis, and oxalate nephropathy; lumasiran (an RNAi against glycolate oxidase) lowers urinary oxalate substantially.

citations.title: Cardiovascular-Kidney-Metabolic (CKM) Syndrome
citations.snippet: The AHA cardiovascular-kidney-metabolic (CKM) framework stages overlapping obesity, diabetes, CKD, and CVD risk and promotes coordinated therapy (SGLT2i, GLP-1, RASi, statins) across the continuum.

citations.title: ACEi Adverse Effects
citations.section: Cough, angioedema, and switching to ARB
citations.snippet: ACE inhibitor cough and angioedema stem from bradykinin; switch to an ARB for cough (ARBs rarely cause angioedema) and never rechallenge ACEi after angioedema. Both raise potassium and creatinine expectedly.

citations.title: Volume Status & Target Weight
citations.snippet: Assess dialysis dry weight clinically (edema, BP, intradialytic symptoms) supplemented by bioimpedance or lung ultrasound; both over- and under-filling drive morbidity, so probe target weight gradually.

citations.title: Obesity-Related Glomerulopathy
citations.snippet: Obesity-related glomerulopathy is a secondary FSGS from glomerular hyperfiltration presenting with subnephrotic proteinuria; treat with weight loss, RAS blockade, and SGLT2i rather than immunosuppression.

citations.title: HIV-Associated Nephropathy
citations.snippet: HIV-associated nephropathy (collapsing FSGS, more common with APOL1 risk variants) responds to antiretroviral therapy plus RAS blockade; consider biopsy to distinguish HIVAN from immune-complex disease.

citations.title: APOL1-Mediated Kidney Disease
citations.snippet: Two APOL1 risk alleles substantially raise risk of FSGS, HIVAN, and hypertension-attributed CKD in people of recent African ancestry; APOL1 inhibitors are in development and testing informs risk and donor evaluation.

citations.title: AKI Phenotyping in the Hospital
citations.snippet: Approach inpatient AKI systematically: assess volume/perfusion (prerenal), review nephrotoxins and sepsis (ATN/AIN), examine urine, and rule out obstruction — most hospital AKI is multifactorial ATN and prerenal.

citations.title: Proteinuria Evaluation
citations.snippet: Quantify proteinuria (UACR/UPCR), distinguish glomerular from tubular/overflow patterns, and pursue serologies and biopsy when nephrotic-range or unexplained; nephrotic-range proteinuria signals glomerular disease.

citations.title: Kidney Biopsy — Indications
citations.section: When to biopsy the native kidney
citations.snippet: Consider native kidney biopsy for unexplained AKI, nephrotic syndrome, glomerular hematuria with proteinuria, or suspected systemic disease with renal involvement, weighing bleeding risk and whether results will change management.
